title: Clinical Guidance for Acute Rheumatic Fever | Group A Strep | CDC
text:

Therapy for acute rheumatic fever includes salicylates and anti-inflammatory medicines to relieve inflammation and decrease fever.
Antibiotics 
These patients should also be started on antibiotics to eliminate any residual group A strep bacteria. Treatment is indicated regardless of the presence or absence of pharyngitis or positive Strep test at the time of diagnosis.
Prognosis and complications 
Prognosis 
Untreated rheumatic fever increases a person's risk of recurrent attacks and worsens prognosis. Prognosis is related to the prevention of recurrent attacks, degree of cardiac valvular damage, and degree of overall cardiac involvement.
Complications 
Rheumatic heart disease is the most important long-term sequela of acute rheumatic fever. It can cause disability or death.
Cardiac complications may vary in severity and include, but aren't limited to:
Arrhythmias 
Congestive heart failure 
Endocarditis 
Pericarditis 
Stroke 
Valvular damage 
Prevention 
Recall alert 
On July 10, 2025 , King Pharmaceuticals, a subsidiary of Pfizer, issued a voluntary recall of specific referenced lots of Bicillin® L-A (Penicillin G Benzathine Injectable Suspension). 
Find further details and potential mitigation measures for health departments and healthcare providers during a period of limited supply of Bicillin® L-A: Availability of STI Testing & Treatment Products
